title: Add positional encoding to associate lab codes with corresponding values
body:
The attention mechanism in transformers treats all tokens as equally related, without any built-in notion of which tokens are "next to" each other. This makes it difficult for the model to understand which lab value corresponds to which lab code.

Proposal:

Introduce an additional positional or relational encoding to explicitly link each lab value with its corresponding lab code, through a shared embedding.

title: Exclude nan is removing events on additional columns
body:
Description:
In some cases, the MEDS table contains additional columns that include None or NaN values not relevant to feature creation. These extra columns can unintentionally cause rows to be dropped when applying NaN filtering.

To address this, we now restrict the concepts DataFrame to only the relevant columns — subject_id, time, and code — before processing. This ensures that NaN filtering is applied only where appropriate and prevents accidental loss of valid events.

title: Ensure birthdate token is present
body:
If "birthdate" is missing in MEDS, data creation continues without error, leading to incorrect [background sequence](https://github.com/FGA-DIKU/EHR/blob/65191eba9566cb041e19334306517074bf5968b1/corebehrt/functional/features/creators.py#L157) and possible censoring failures.

Suggestion:

Add a check for "birthdate" during data creation.

Also, allow specifying required background codes (e.g., "gender") via config or enforce presence of at least one "BG_" code.

## Description

The `create_background()` function uses index-based selection to update `TIMESTAMP_COL` values. This approach leads to incorrect behavior when the `concepts` DataFrame contains **duplicate or non-contiguous indices**.

title: **Title: Exclude Specific Codes from Delayed Censoring**
body:
**Current**

```yaml
concept_pattern_hours_delay:
  "^D": 72
```

**Goal**
Delay all diagnosis codes by 72 h *except* lung-cancer codes (`DC*`), which should be censored immediately.

**Proposed**

```yaml
concept_pattern_hours_delay:
  codes:
    "^D": 72      # 72 h delay for diagnoses
  exclude:
    - "^D/DC.*"   # immediate censoring for lung-cancer codes
```

**Logic**

1. If code matches any `exclude` pattern → delay = 0
2. Else if it matches a `codes` pattern → apply specified delay
3. E

## Feature Request

Add a button in the GUI dashboard to access and view stored tokens/PII mappings.

## Problem

When PrivacyGuardian tokenizes sensitive data (like passwords), users need a way to retrieve the original values. Currently there's no GUI way to look up what a token like `◈PG:PASS_fa3ea86129f9◈` maps to.

**Bigger problem:** Even if you can see the values, without context you won't know *what* each piece of data is for. A password is useless if you don't know which service it belon

title: Descriptive Tokens: The Borrowed Tongue Architecture
body:
## Summary

Extend PrivacyGuardian from PII-only protection to **full semantic tokenization** with descriptive metadata hints.

**Goal:** AI understands code structure and patterns without seeing actual sensitive values.

## The Problem

Current approach only protects PII patterns (emails, passwords, API keys). But:
- Function names like `connect_to_acme_production` leak company identity
- Hostnames like `db.acme-corp.internal` leak infrastructure
- Comments often contain sensitive context

## T

title: Implement LD_PRELOAD hook to protect GUI apps
body:
## Summary

Protect GUI apps (Antigravity, Cursor, Windsurf) by intercepting data BEFORE encryption, not at the network level.

## Learning Doc

Read first: [docs/learnings/0005-ld-preload-ssl-hook.md](https://github.com/Keeper888/privacyguardian/blob/main/docs/learnings/0005-ld-preload-ssl-hook.md)

## The Idea

```
App prepares request with PII
       ↓
LD_PRELOAD hooks SSL_write ← WE INTERCEPT HERE
       ↓
Filter PII, replace with tokens
       ↓
App encrypts already-filtered data
       ↓
S

title: Auto-start on boot (systemd service)

## Overview
PrivacyGuardian must start automatically when the OS boots, before any LLM apps launch.

## Implementation

### 1. Systemd User Service
```ini
# ~/.config/systemd/user/privacyguardian.service
[Unit]
Description=PrivacyGuardian PII Protection Proxy
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/home/raven/Desktop/ClaudeVault/Projects/privacyguardian/guardian start
Restart=always
RestartSec=3

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

## Summary

title: refactor: Implement MaxOntSim@K Metric for Benchmark Evaluation
body:
**Description:**
The current Ontology Similarity metric (`OntSim@K`) calculates the *average* of the maximum similarities found for each expected term within the top K results. This doesn't align with the user requirement to capture the single *best* semantic match achieved between *any* expected term and *any* top K retrieved term, similar to how Hit Rate captures the best exact match.

This issue tracks the implementation of a new metric, "Maximum Ontology Similarity @ K" (`MaxOntSim@K`), whic

title: SQLite threading issue with CTG client caching
body:
## Problem

The ClinicalTrials.gov (CTG) API client uses `hishel.requests.CacheAdapter()` for HTTP caching, which creates SQLite connections that are thread-local. However, the CTG client uses `asyncio.to_thread()` to run synchronous `requests` calls in separate threads, causing SQLite threading errors:

```
SQLite objects created in a thread can only be used in that same thread. The object was created in thread id 6285668352 and this is thread id 6319321088.
```

- `ctg_searc

title: Disable open registration - security risk
body:
Anyone with API link can create account and access all data - big security hole for self-hosted deployments.

I see two options for now:
- Hide behind auth - require logged-in developer to create users. Here I see the problem that no one wants to set passwords on behalf of users.
- Remove endpoint completely - cleaner approach, add invite flow later (admin sends email -> user sets own password)

I would go with second approach - less code to maintain now, proper invite system comes later

title: TypeError: AppleWorkouts missing abstract method implementations causing /api/v1/oauth/providers endpoint failure
body:
The GET /api/v1/oauth/providers endpoint returns a 500 error when attempting to retrieve the list of providers. The error occurs during initialization of AppleStrategy, which tries to create an instance of AppleWorkouts.

`TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class AppleWorkouts without an implementation for abstract methods '_extract_dates', 'get_workout_detail_from_api', 'get_workouts_from_api', 'load_data'` 

We don't need to have those methods to return Apple as provider.

title: `X-Open-Wearables-API-Key` authentication not working / inconsistent across endpoints
body:
API Key authentication via X-Open-Wearables-API-Key header doesn't seem to work properly and the OpenAPI spec is inconsistent about which endpoints support it.

**Auth not working where documented:**
`POST /api/v1/users` has `X-Open-Wearables-API-Key` defined as optional header param in the spec, but requests with valid API key return 401. Only OAuth2 bearer token works.

**Auth not documented (and likely not supported) where expected:**
`GET /api/v1/users` doesn't have `X-Open-Wearables-API-Key
